reasoning: The United States 10-Year Treasury yield has not reached 5% between December 2024 and June 2025. The peak during this period occurred in January 2025, reaching around 4.85%. Subsequently, the yield has declined and fluctuated in the mid-4% range. As of June 20, 2025, just days before the resolution date, the yield sits near a stable 4.35% to 4.40%. The chance of the 10-year yield surging more than 60 basis points to cross the 5.0% threshold in the remaining 8 days before the market closes on June 30 is negligible absent an unprecedented, cataclysmic economic shock. The market essentially priced this out (Polymarket at 4% on June 12), and yields have remained flat or slightly declined since then. Thus, the probability is comfortably at the 2% lower bound.
